title: Preparation of LRRK2 RCKW cryo-EM grids
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e0vk6","text":"This is Leschziner's Lab updated protocol for making cryo-EM grids for LRRK2 RCKW. This protocol, when using lower protein concentration, results in better monomer and dimer formation than the old protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

7.4","unit":28}},"1":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"","data":{"concentration":"80","guid":"A72EECE0636111EBB4106FFB24F69D20","id":0,"label":"NaCl","unit":28}},"2":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"","data":{"concentration":"0.5","guid":"BB6B6FD0636111EBB4106FFB24F69D20","id":0,"label":"TCEP","unit":28}},"3":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"","data":{"concentration":"5","guid":"C761E030636111EBB4106FFB24F69D20","id":0,"label":"Glycerol","unit":14}},"4":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"","data":{"concentration":"2.5","guid":"D3C54330636111EBB4106FFB24F69D20","id":0,"label":"MgCl2","unit":28}},"5":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"","data":{"concentration":"20","guid":"E07343C0636111EBB4106FFB24F69D20","id":0,"label":"GDP","unit":29}}}}
steps: Plasma clean grids. We used UltrAuFoil Holey Gold 1.2/1.3 300 mesh grids and plasma cleaned them in the Solarus II (Gatan) using the QuantiFoil Au preset.
Apply protein to grids and plunge freeze. We used a Vitrobot (FEI) to blot away excess sample and plunge freeze
Store grids in liquid nitrogen until ready for imaging.
Spin down purified LRRK2 RCKW. 10000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes, (can be faster) Leave protein on ice afterwards. For best results, reduce the amount of time between spinning and freezing samples.

Dilute samples to desired concentration in the LRRK2 buffer . Make sure final salt is at 80 mM NaCl. For best results, make 10 µL samples, good for freezing 2 grids. This is to minimize time spent outside of storage buffer, reducing aggregation. High concentrations favor dimers and trimers, while lower concentrations favor monomers.
(Optional) If using inhibitors, let them incubate on ice in the final LRRK2 buffer before plunge freezing.
creator: Mariusz Matyszewski
